name: release-skills
description: Release workflow for baoyu-skills plugin. Use when user says "release", "发布", "push", "推送", "new version", "新版本", "bump version", "更新版本", or wants to publish changes to remote. Analyzes changes since last tag, updates CHANGELOG (EN/CN), bumps marketplace.json version, commits, and creates version tag. MUST be used before any git push with uncommitted skill changes.
capabilities: Analyze git changes since last release tag
Auto-detect version bump type (patch, minor, major)
Generate changelog entries in English and Chinese
Create git commit and version tag
Preview all changes with dry-run mode
author: jimliu
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill. Static analyzer flagged shell command examples and hash symbols in markdown as potential security issues, but these are harmless documentation patterns. No executable code, network access, or file operations exist in this skill.
valueStatement: Managing plugin releases manually leads to forgotten changelog updates and inconsistent versioning. This skill automates the entire release workflow including version bump detection, changelog generation, and git tag creation.
limitations: Only works within baoyu-skills repository structure
Does not push to remote repositories (manual push required)
No support for pre-release versions (reserved for future)

useCases.title: Quick Patch Release
useCases.description: Run the skill to automatically bump patch version, update changelogs, and create a release tag for bug fix releases.

useCases.title: Feature Release with Changelog
useCases.description: Prepare a minor release with properly formatted changelog entries in both English and Chinese for new features.

useCases.title: Release Preview Before Publishing
useCases.description: Use dry-run mode to preview exactly what will change before committing, ensuring release accuracy.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Release
promptTemplates.prompt: Run /release-skills to create a release for the current changes.
promptTemplates.scenario: Standard release with auto-detected version bump

promptTemplates.title: Preview Release Changes
promptTemplates.prompt: Run /release-skills --dry-run to see what changes would be made without actually committing.

promptTemplates.title: Minor Version Release
promptTemplates.prompt: Run /release-skills --minor to force a minor version bump for new features.
promptTemplates.scenario: Releasing new features that warrant minor version increment

promptTemplates.title: Major Version Release
promptTemplates.prompt: Run /release-skills --major to create a major version release.
promptTemplates.scenario: Releasing breaking changes that require major version bump

bestPractices: Always run with --dry-run first to verify changes before committing
Review changelog translations for accuracy before final release
Use --major flag only when breaking changes are explicitly required
antiPatterns: Skipping the release workflow and pushing directly without version bump
Updating only one language changelog (always sync EN and CN)
Forgetting to push tags after pushing main branch

faq.question: Does this skill push to remote?
faq.answer: No. The skill creates commits and tags locally. You must run 'git push' manually after the release completes.

faq.question: How does version bump detection work?
faq.answer: The skill analyzes commit messages. Feat: prefixes trigger minor bumps, all other changes trigger patch bumps unless forced with --major or --minor.

faq.question: Can I release pre-release versions?
faq.answer: The --pre flag is reserved but not yet implemented. Currently only supports stable releases.

faq.question: What if I have breaking changes?
faq.answer: Use --major flag to force a major version bump. The skill will warn you if breaking changes are detected.

faq.question: Does this work for non-baoyu projects?
faq.answer: No. This skill is specifically designed for the baoyu-skills repository structure with its specific file locations.

faq.question: How are changelogs formatted?
faq.answer: Changelogs use Keep a Changelog format with sections for Features, Fixes, Documentation, and Other. Only sections with changes are included.
